CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-08T07:34:36.4345336Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T07:32:07.888223547Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57921,\"title\":\"[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document conditional preview API version filtering in ResourceVersions\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 21456f8 (#57862) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nCommit 21456f8 added conditional preview API version filtering in `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`. Previously, `ResourceVersions` may have included preview versions even when the default was GA. Now:\\n\\n- **If the default API version is GA** → only GA versions are listed in `ResourceVersions` (previews are excluded)\\n- **If the default API version is itself a preview** → all versions (GA + preview) are listed\\n\\nThe generated test project files (`ConfigurationStore.cs`, `Item.cs`, `KeyValue.cs`) were updated to reflect this new filtering, and `sdk/batch/Azure.Provisioning.Batch/tsp-location.yaml` was updated to point to the commit that introduced this behavior.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` — The \\u0026#34;ResourceVersions Nested Class\\u0026#34; section says *\\u0026#34;Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order\\u0026#34;* without explaining that preview versions **are** included when the default version is itself a preview.\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` — Section 6 \\u0026#34;Output Types\\u0026#34; describes `ResourceVersions` only as *\\u0026#34;nested class with GA API version constants\\u0026#34;*, omitting the conditional logic that governs which versions are included.\\n- Neither document explains what constitutes a \\u0026#34;preview\\u0026#34; version (i.e., the `IsPreviewApiVersion` heuristic — version strings containing `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`, etc.).\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md`\\n\\nEdit the \\u0026#34;ResourceVersions Nested Class\\u0026#34; section. Locate the paragraph:\\n\\n```markdown\\nLists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order:\\n```\\n\\nReplace it with:\\n\\n```markdown\\nLists supported API versions in reverse chronological order. The set of included versions depends on the default (latest) API version for the resource:\\n\\n- **When the default version is GA** — only GA versions are included; preview versions are filtered out.\\n- **When the default version is itself a preview** — all versions (GA and preview) are included.\\n\\nA version is considered a preview if its string contains `preview`, `alpha`, or `beta` (case-insensitive).\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md`\\n\\nIn Section 6 \\u0026#34;Output Types\\u0026#34; → \\u0026#34;Resource Provider\\u0026#34; bullet list, locate:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with GA API version constants\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with API version constants. When the default (latest) API version is GA, only GA versions are included (previews are filtered out). When the default is itself a preview, all versions (GA + preview) are included.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo snippet changes required — these are design/spec documents, not README files using the snippet toolchain.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThese are internal design documents (`docs/design.md`, `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md`) for a generator package, not a user-facing SDK README. No standard section structure check applies.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands from the repo root. Only the first command applies since there are no C# or test file changes:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/generator/` — confirm generator still builds cleanly after doc edits (no code changes, so this is a sanity check).\\n\\nNo snippet update or API export script is needed for documentation-only edits.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24122942156/agentic_workflow) · ● 961.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57921\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-08T07:28:06Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-08T07:28:06Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-08T07:34:36.4495706Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T07:32:42.078539252Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":23,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4139581423,\"number\":57489,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Provisioning Generator] ARM resource common properties should be emitted before other properties\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator emits ARM resource properties in a different order than the old reflection-based generator. Specifically, `properties` is emitted before `name` and `location`, which changes the Bicep output ordering.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe old reflection-based generator sorted properties with this ordering logic (from `Specification.Analyze.cs`):\\n```csharp\\nresource.Properties.OrderBy(p =\\u003e (p.Name == \\\"Name\\\" ? 0 : p.IsReadOnly ? '3' : p.IsRequired ? '1' : '2') + p.Name)\\n```\\n\\nThis ensured `name` was always first, followed by required properties alphabetically, then read-only/output properties.\\n\\nGenerated `DefineProvisionableProperties()`:\\n```csharp\\n_name = DefineProperty\\u003cstring\\u003e(\\\"Name\\\", [\\\"name\\\"], isRequired: true);\\n_location = DefineProperty\\u003cAzureLocation\\u003e(\\\"Location\\\", [\\\"location\\\"], isRequired: true);\\n_properties = DefineModelProperty\\u003cKeyVaultProperties\\u003e(\\\"Properties\\\", [\\\"properties\\\"], isRequired: true);\\n```\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nThe new generator emits:\\n```csharp\\n_properties = DefineModelProperty\\u003cKeyVaultProperties\\u003e(nameof(Properties), new string[] { \\\"properties\\\" }, isRequired: true);\\n_name = DefineProperty\\u003cstring\\u003e(nameof(Name), new string[] { \\\"name\\\" }, isRequired: true);\\n_location = DefineProperty\\u003cAzureLocation\\u003e(nameof(Location), new string[] { \\\"location\\\" }, isRequired: true);\\n```\\n\\nSince the Bicep emitter uses insertion order, this causes `properties:` to appear before `name:` in the Bicep output.\\n\\n### Impact\\n\\nAll regenerated provisioning libraries will have a different Bicep property ordering than before. While functionally equivalent, this is a behavioral change that breaks existing tests and differs from standard Bicep conventions where `name` and `location` typically appear before `properties`.\\n\\n### Proposed fix\\n\\nThe provisioning generator should ensure ARM resource common properties (`name`, `location`, `tags`) are always defined before the `properties` bag and other custom properties in `DefineProvisionableProperties()`.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTU0NDQ2\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1508411177,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/CodeGen\",\"name\":\"CodeGen\",\"color\":\"e99695\",\"description\":\"Issues Generator] Child resource location property incorrectly marked as required instead of output-only\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe provisioning generator incorrectly marks `location` as `isRequired: true` for child resources (e.g., `KeyVaultSecret`) when the TypeSpec definition has `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)` (read-only) and is optional.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nFor `Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/secrets`, the TypeSpec definition is:\\n```typespec\\n@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)\\nlocation?: string;\\n```\\n\\nThe old reflection-based generator correctly generated:\\n```csharp\\n_location = DefineProperty\\u003cAzureLocation\\u003e(\\\"Location\\\", [\\\"location\\\"], isOutput: true);\\n```\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nThe new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator generates:\\n```csharp\\n_location = DefineProperty\\u003cAzureLocation\\u003e(nameof(Location), new string[] { \\\"location\\\" }, isRequired: true);\\n```\\n\\nThis causes `location: location` to appear in the Bicep output for child resources, which is incorrect — the [Bicep reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.keyvault/vaults/secrets?pivots=deployment-language-bicep) confirms `location` is not a settable property on `vaults/secrets`.\\n\\n### TypeSpec reference\\n\\nhttps://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/[REDACTED]/specification/keyvault/resource-manager/Microsoft.KeyVault/KeyVault/Secret.tsp\\n\\n### Impact\\n\\nAny provisioning library regenerated with the new generator will emit spurious `location` properties for child generator to the new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator (`http-client-csharp-provisioning`).\\n\\nOnly provisioning libraries whose peer management library has already been migrated to TypeSpec (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`) are eligible for migration. generation\",\"default\":false,\"node_id\":\"MDU6TGFiZWwxNTA4NDExMTc3\"},{\"id\":6560170331,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Provisioning\",\"name\":\"Provisioning\",\"color\":\"ffeb77\",\"description\":\"\",\"default\":false,\"node_id\":\"LA_kwDOACyxMM8AAAABhwRBWw\"}],\"comments\":0,\"created_at\":\"2026-03-18T03:07:39Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-18T03:07:54Z\",\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57205\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57205\",\"comments_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57205/comments\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57205/events\",\"labels_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57205/labels{/name}\",\"repository_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57205/reactions\"},\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM7z7Tzx\"},{\"id\":4087046352,\"number\":57165,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Provisioning Generator] dotnet build /t:GenerateCode fails due to missing transitive dependency\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\n`dotnet build /t:GenerateCode` fails for provisioning SDK packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault`) because the published provisioning emitter package is missing `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager` as a dependency.\\n\\n## Error\\n\\n```\\nCannot find package '@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager' imported from\\n.../node_modules/@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt/dist/emitter/resolve-arm-resources-converter.js\\n```\\n\\nFollowed by TypeSpec compilation errors (`Unknown identifier ResourceNameParameter`, `Unknown decorator @armResourceOperations`, etc.)\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe provisioning emitter package.json (`eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-provisioning-emitter-package.json`) only declares:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"dependencies\\\": {\\n    \\\"@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning\\\": \\\"1.0.0-alpha.20260316.3\\\"\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe provisioning emitter transitively depends on `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`, which imports `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager` in `resolve-arm-resources-converter.js`. However, this transitive dependency is not resolved when `tsp-client update` runs `npm ci` in the `TempTypeSpecFiles` directory.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nUse `RegenSdkLocal.ps1` instead of `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`:\\n\\n```powershell\\npwsh eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/eng/scripts/RegenSdkLocal.ps1 -Services \\\"KeyVault\\\"\\n```\\n\\nThis works because it builds the emitter locally with all dependencies available.\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\n`dotnet build /t:GenerateCode` should work for provisioning SDK packages, just as it works for management SDK packages.\\n\\n## Found In\\n\\nProvisioning Migration - KeyVault
